summary:
Hello World

A sample go program is show here.

package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
  message := greetMe("world")
  fmt.Println(message)
}

func greetMe(name string) string {
  return "Hello, " + name + "!"
}

Run the program as below:

$ go run hello.go
Variables

Normal Declaration:

var msg string
msg = "Hello"

Shortcut:

msg := "Hello"
Constants
const Phi = 1.618
title: Introduction

contents: This article is published on Medium. Open the link to read the full piece.
summary: This article is published on Medium. Open the link to read the full piece.
title: Dealing with Masters Applications — Part 1

contents:
Overview Developed an R-based soil moisture balance model to simulate daily crop water use and accurately estimate irrigation demand across a 100,000+ acre agricultural basin in California. The model integrated climate, crop, soil, and land use data to dynamically track soil moisture and drive water demand calculations.
Groundwater and MODFLOW To evaluate groundwater sustainability, I executed MODFLOW 6 simulations using FloPy, modeling various pumping scenarios to assess spatial and temporal stress on the aquifer system. This analysis supported decision-making for one of California’s most over-drafted groundwater basins, providing key insights for sustainable water management strategies under SGMA, including conjunctive use, managed aquifer recharge, and demand reduction planning.
summary:
Overview

Developed an R-based soil moisture balance model to simulate daily crop water use and accurately estimate irrigation demand across a 100,000+ acre agricultural basin in California. The model integrated climate, crop, soil, and land use data to dynamically track soil moisture and drive water demand calculations.

Groundwater and MODFLOW

To evaluate groundwater sustainability, I executed MODFLOW 6 simulations using FloPy, modeling various pumping scenarios to assess spatial and temporal stress on the aquifer system. This analysis supported decision-making for one of California’s most over-drafted groundwater basins, providing key insights for sustainable water management strategies under SGMA, including conjunctive use, managed aquifer recharge, and demand reduction planning.
title: Soil Moisture Balance Model

contents:
Overview Spearheaded a six-month startup incubation project with a team of five to develop a carbon management app, overseeing program management, team onboarding, milestone tracking, and resource allocation while implementing incentives through discounts and gamification.
Outreach Presented the concept at TreeHacks 2023 and the Stanford Graduate School of Business Ignite Program in 2023.
summary:
Overview

Spearheaded a six-month startup incubation project with a team of five to develop a carbon management app, overseeing program management, team onboarding, milestone tracking, and resource allocation while implementing incentives through discounts and gamification.

Outreach

Presented the concept at TreeHacks 2023 and the Stanford Graduate School of Business Ignite Program in 2023.
title: CARMA - Startup Simulation Project

contents:
Overview Analyzed climate change impacts on Central California water infrastructure using GIS and DWR data to assess basin vulnerabilities. Provided data-driven insights that supported five regional water management plans.
Resilience index Led a team of three in developing a Python script computing a dynamic resilience index for San Luis Obispo County, integrating 10+ data sources on water demand, supply, and climate projections to enhance drought preparedness efforts.
summary:
Overview

Analyzed climate change impacts on Central California water infrastructure using GIS and DWR data to assess basin vulnerabilities. Provided data-driven insights that supported five regional water management plans.

Resilience index

Led a team of three in developing a Python script computing a dynamic resilience index for San Luis Obispo County, integrating 10+ data sources on water demand, supply, and climate projections to enhance drought preparedness efforts.
title: Water Resiliency Model for Central Coast California

contents:
Overview Performed frequency analysis on stream flows and precipitation in northern Ohio using the Vogel et al. (2011) method, utilizing 76 years of data from 133 stream gauges.
Findings Assessed the impact of trends on extreme events over the next 20 years for 100- to 500-year return periods through the application of magnification factors and reduced return periods.
summary:
Overview

Performed frequency analysis on stream flows and precipitation in northern Ohio using the Vogel et al. (2011) method, utilizing 76 years of data from 133 stream gauges.

Findings

Assessed the impact of trends on extreme events over the next 20 years for 100- to 500-year return periods through the application of magnification factors and reduced return periods.
title: Frequency Analysis of Non-Stationary Hydrological Extremes in Northern Ohio

contents: Overview Analyzed the environmental and economic viability of geothermal energy for data centers. Conducted a comparative analysis between electricity from the grid and geothermal energy along with a waste recovery system, and presented a poster with findings and recommendations.
summary:
Overview

Analyzed the environmental and economic viability of geothermal energy for data centers. Conducted a comparative analysis between electricity from the grid and geothermal energy along with a waste recovery system, and presented a poster with findings and recommendations.
title: Life Cycle Assessment of Geothermal Energy for Data Centers

contents: Overview Built a scoping framework that involved reviewing the current energy mix, policies, and potential for future technologies across countries to develop a scenario for creating a sustainable energy system for 9 billion people in 2050 in accordance with meeting Paris Agreement targets.
summary:
Overview

Built a scoping framework that involved reviewing the current energy mix, policies, and potential for future technologies across countries to develop a scenario for creating a sustainable energy system for 9 billion people in 2050 in accordance with meeting Paris Agreement targets.
title: Sustainable Energy Model for 2050

contents:
Overview Executed a data-driven flood analysis using concepts of expected loss and exceedance probability along with 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations.
Outcomes Proposed a hybrid solution combining insurance and levees, reducing potential annual losses by 40% and eliminating risk of $400K+ damages.
summary:
Overview

Executed a data-driven flood analysis using concepts of expected loss and exceedance probability along with 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations.

Outcomes

Proposed a hybrid solution combining insurance and levees, reducing potential annual losses by 40% and eliminating risk of $400K+ damages.
title: Flood Mitigation Analysis for Foster City
